zh-tw.title: 畢業語錄
zh-tw.description: 從搞笑到感人，從告別到出發——精選畢業季最有共鳴的語錄，適合IG、LINE分享或送給即將踏入新階段的你

en.title: Graduation Quotes
en.description: Funny, heartfelt, and real — a curated collection of graduation quotes for captions, messages, and moments that mark the end of one chapter and the start of the next

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
終於畢業了。

這代表我終於可以換一個地方，
繼續過著不確定自己在幹嘛的生活。

但至少換了一個新地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給剛畢業的朋友或貼到IG，比嚴肅的畢業祝賀更接地氣，也更讓人有共鳴——尤其是那些還不知道畢業後要幹嘛的人
variations: 畢業是人生的一個里程碑，里程碑之後你會發現：路還是一樣要繼續走，只是換了一條不認識的。

en.content:
Finally graduated.

Which means I can now move on to being confused
in an entirely new setting.

Progress.
en.editorNote: Post this on Instagram or send to a freshly graduated friend — more relatable than formal congratulations, especially for the ones who still have no idea what comes next
variations: Graduation is a milestone. After the milestone, you realize the road still goes on — just into territory you've never seen before.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
告別的，不只是學校。

是那個每天都有人等你的早晨。
是那個在教室外面傳訊息說「你到了嗎」的人。
是那段不用解釋就知道你在想什麼的時光。

以後還是可以聯絡，但那個「每天」，真的結束了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在畢業典禮後傳給一起走過這段路的朋友，或是貼在IG配上畢業照，引發深刻共鳴
variations: 最難說再見的不是地方，是那個在那個地方、只屬於那段時間的我們。

en.content:
You're not just saying goodbye to a school.

You're saying goodbye to the mornings when someone was always waiting for you.
To the message that said "are you here yet?" before class.
To the kind of silence that didn't need explaining.

You can still keep in touch. But the 'every day' part — that's really over.
en.editorNote: Send to the friends who walked through it with you, or post with a graduation photo — hits differently than a formal farewell
variations: The hardest goodbye isn't the place. It's the version of us that only existed there.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
「我們以後一定要常聯絡！」
「一定要！」

——六個月後——

「……你還好嗎？」
「對，你呢？」
「還好。」
「好。」
「好。」

友情就這樣傳承下去了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你在畢業典禮上說「要常聯絡」的朋友，誰先傳誰就贏了
variations: 有一種友情叫做：明明好幾個月沒說話，但只要其中一個人先傳，就馬上回到熟悉的頻率。

en.content:
"We have to keep in touch!"
"Definitely!"

— Six months later —

"...Hey, you doing okay?"
"Yeah. You?"
"Yeah."
"Cool."
"Cool."

And that's friendship, carried forward.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend you said 'let's keep in touch' to at graduation — whoever texts first wins
variations: There's a kind of friendship where months can pass in silence, but the moment one of you reaches out, you're both immediately back at the same frequency.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作（社畜era）

zh-tw.content:
恭喜你，畢業了。

學生era正式結束。
社畜era即將解鎖。
中間這段不知道要幹嘛的era，
請好好珍惜。

它通常很短，而且不會再來了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業典禮前後傳給同學，也可以貼到IG，「In My ___ Era」格式在2026是超流行的IG caption風格
variations: In My 剛畢業什麼都不確定 Era — 這是你可以用的IG caption，同時也是事實陳述。

en.content:
Congratulations, you graduated.

Student era: officially closed.
Working adult era: unlocking.
The in-between era where you have no idea what you're doing:
please treasure it.

It's usually short. And it doesn't come back.
en.editorNote: Post at graduation season — the 'In My ___ Era' format is a dominant 2026 Instagram caption style and this version nails it
variations: In My Freshly Graduated Everything Is Uncertain Era — both a valid IG caption and an accurate description of your current state.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
世界很大。

先出去走走再說。

不用現在就知道要去哪裡，
只要先邁出去就好。
方向，走著走著就清楚了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些畢業後還在猶豫、還不確定下一步的人——不說教，只是給一個出發的理由
variations: 你不需要有計畫才能開始，只需要一個不想繼續待在原地的理由。

en.content:
The world is big.

Go explore first, figure it out later.

You don't need to know where you're going right now.
Just take the first step.
The direction gets clearer as you walk.
en.editorNote: For graduates who are hesitating or uncertain about what comes next — not preachy, just a reason to start moving
variations: You don't need a plan to begin. You just need a reason strong enough to stop staying where you are.

items.sourceNote: 畢業英文IG caption，重新創作

zh-tw.content: 流蘇終於值了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 翻譯自英文 caption 名句「The tassel was worth the hassle」，簡短有力，配上學士帽照片效果一流
variations: 四年的努力，換一頂帽子和一張紙。但那頂帽子，拍起來真的很好看。

en.content: The tassel was worth the hassle.
en.editorNote: One of the most shareable graduation captions of all time — works as a standalone photo caption or printed on a card
variations: Four years for a piece of paper and a hat. The hat photographs really well, though.

items.sourceNote: 畢業英文IG caption，重新創作

zh-tw.content: 沒在開玩笑，我真的畢業了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 輕鬆不做作的畢業po文，適合不想說大道理但還是想記錄這一刻的人，英文版「No cap, I actually graduated」更潮
variations: 就算我自己也不敢相信，但學校說我畢業了，我就相信學校的判斷。

en.content: No cap, I actually graduated.
en.editorNote: Casual, unfiltered, and completely relatable — perfect for people who want to mark the moment without being dramatic about it
variations: Even I didn't see this coming. But the diploma says so, so I'll take it.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
謝謝那些跟我一起熬過期末考的人。

不一定每次都考好，
但每次你傳來「你考完了嗎」的訊息，
我就覺得還有人在等我，
再爛的考試也可以撐過去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那些在考試期間一直陪著你、互相催眠說「會過的」的人——這句話比任何祝賀都更讓人感到被記住
variations: 能一起讀書不一定重要，重要的是你知道考完之後有人等你去吃東西。

en.content:
Thank you to everyone who survived finals with me.

We didn't always score well,
but every time you texted 'are you done yet?'
I knew someone was waiting.
And somehow that made even the worst exams bearable.
en.editorNote: Send to the people who spent late nights studying beside you — this lands deeper than any formal thank-you
variations: Studying together mattered less than knowing someone was waiting for you to go eat after the exam.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你知道畢業代表什麼嗎？

代表你通過了所有的考試、所有的死線、所有那些你說「這次真的沒救了」的時刻。

既然這些都過了，
剩下的人生還有什麼是過不去的？

（應該是有，但現在不用想這麼多。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業後的各種慶祝場合都能說這句，讓大家先開心一下——括號裡的那句是給想太多的人加的
variations: 你連畢業論文都熬過來了。你已經沒有什麼好怕的了。（先信這個，其他的以後再說。）

en.content:
You know what graduating means?

It means you survived every test, every deadline, every moment you said 'I'm completely done for this time.'

If you made it through all of that,
what's left in life that you can't get through?

(There are things. But you don't need to think about them right now.)
en.editorNote: Works at any graduation celebration — gives everyone a moment of genuine confidence, with the parenthetical for people who overthink everything
variations: You survived your thesis. There is nothing left to fear. (Believe this for now. Deal with the rest later.)

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，你不一定知道下一步要去哪裡。

但你已經不是剛入學時的那個人了。

光是這一點，就很值得慶祝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些在畢業典禮上感覺自己還是沒想清楚的人——不需要有答案，改變本身就是答案
variations: 你不需要畢業就馬上知道人生方向。你只需要知道：你比四年前準備得更好了。

en.content:
After graduation, you may not know where you're going next.

But you are no longer the person who walked in on the first day.

That alone is worth celebrating.
en.editorNote: For the person who still doesn't have it all figured out at graduation — you don't need the answers; the change itself is the answer
variations: You don't need to know your life's direction the day you graduate. You just need to know: you're more ready than you were four years ago.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
謝謝那個在你說「我讀不下去了」的時候，
沒有叫你繼續撐，
只說了一句「先休息吧」的人。

他可能不知道，就是那一句話，
讓你撐到了今天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個陪你走過這段路的人——家人、朋友、伴侶都適用，不說教，只是讓他們知道那些小事都有被記住
variations: 有一種支持，不是激勵你，而是在你快撐不住的時候，讓你知道有人在。

en.content:
Thank you to the person who, when you said 'I can't do this anymore,'
didn't tell you to keep pushing.
They just said, 'take a break.'

They may not know it,
but that one sentence is part of why you made it here.
en.editorNote: Send to whoever kept you going — family, friends, partners — not with motivation, but with presence
variations: Some support isn't about pushing you forward. It's about letting you know someone's there when you're about to fall.

items.sourceNote: 畢業英文IG caption，重新創作

zh-tw.content: 我還在摸索，但我畢業了——這也算是一種進度。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得自己畢業了但好像還沒準備好的人，比「我準備好迎接未來了」更誠實，也更有共鳴
variations: 「我不確定接下來要做什麼，但我確定我從這裡出發了」——這是一種誠實的畢業宣言。

en.content: Still figuring it out — but I graduated, so that counts as progress.
en.editorNote: For everyone who graduates without having it all figured out — more honest than 'ready to take on the world,' and more people will relate
variations: 'I don't know what comes next, but I know I'm leaving from here' — that's an honest graduation statement.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後你才會知道：
學生証是這個世界上最具CP值的身分證。

半價電影、特價博物館、學生方案折扣……

然後就沒了。
請好好珍惜你還有學生証的每一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給即將畢業的學弟妹，讓他們在最後一段學生時光充分利用學生折扣——開玩笑，但是真的
variations: 學生証的失效日，可能是你這輩子最後一次享有正版半價的那一天。慎重對待。

en.content:
After graduation, you'll understand:
A student ID is the highest-value piece of ID ever created.

Half-price movies. Discounted museums. Student plan savings...

And then it's gone.
Treasure every day you still have one.
en.editorNote: Send to underclassmen or juniors still in school — make full use of those discounts while you still can. It's a joke, but it's also real.
variations: Your student ID's expiry date may be the last time you get legal half-price anything. Treat it with respect.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
謝謝那些陪我一起迷路的朋友。

我們不是沒有走錯路，
但至少是一起走錯的。

而且每次走錯，都多認識了彼此一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那些跟你一起選錯課、走錯教室、繞錯路、甚至一起選錯方向的人——那些「一起錯」的時刻，往往才是最難忘的
variations: 不一定要走對路才叫成長，有時候走錯路、但有人一起走，才是這段時間最值錢的部分。

en.content:
Thank you to the friends who got lost with me.

We didn't always take the right turns.
But at least we took the wrong ones together.

And every wrong turn taught us a little more about each other.
en.editorNote: Send to the ones who took the wrong class with you, got confused at the same crossroads, went in the wrong direction at the same time — those 'wrong together' moments are often the most unforgettable
variations: Growth doesn't always require the right path. Sometimes taking the wrong one together is the most valuable part of the whole journey.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
不知道前面是什麼。

但不出發，就永遠不知道。

先走，邊走邊想，
這是目前所有畢業生共同採用的策略。
而且到目前為止，大家都還活著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給所有站在起點、對未來既興奮又不安的畢業生——輕鬆幽默但真心鼓勵，最後一句是廢話文學bonus
variations: 畢業的意義不是你準備好了，而是你決定出發了。這兩件事不一定要同時發生。

en.content:
Nobody knows what's ahead.

But you won't find out by staying.

So start moving. Think while you walk.
This is the shared strategy of every graduate, everywhere.
And so far, everyone has survived.
en.editorNote: For every graduate standing at the starting line feeling both excited and terrified — light humor with genuine encouragement, with a bonus absurd-wisdom final line
variations: Graduating doesn't mean you're ready. It means you decided to go anyway. Those two things don't have to happen at the same time.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Parade 畢業語錄合集，改寫為台灣口語版本

zh-tw.content:
在離開之前，要好好感謝那些默默陪你撐過來的人：

Google。
Ctrl+F。
還有那個幫你找到答案的神奇「複製貼上」。

沒有你們，今天的我不會站在這裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業典禮上唸這段，或是傳給同學當最後的共同回憶——每個人都懂那種靠Google活過考試的感覺
variations: 畢業感言可以很誠實：謝謝所有我在考試前一天發現的YouTube教學影片。

en.content:
Before you go, take a moment to thank those who silently carried you through:

Google.
Ctrl+F.
And the sacred art of copy and paste.

Without you, I would not be standing here today.
en.editorNote: Read this at a graduation party or send it to your study group as a final shared memory — everyone has survived on Google at some point
variations: An honest graduation speech: thank you to every YouTube tutorial I found the night before the exam.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Shutterfly 畢業語錄，改寫為台灣口語風格

zh-tw.content:
很多人在畢業的時候問：「然後呢？」

但其實你不需要現在就知道「然後」。

你只需要知道你想去的那個方向，
然後往那裡走。

「然後呢」，會在路上自己出現的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些在畢業典禮上還是不知道下一步的人——不是說教，只是換一個方式看這個問題
variations: 人生的下一步不一定要現在想清楚。有時候，先動起來，答案才會跟得上你。

en.content:
Everyone asks at graduation: "So what's next?"

But you don't need to know the answer right now.

You just need to know which direction you want to face,
and start walking that way.

The 'what's next' will show up on its own.
en.editorNote: For anyone who still doesn't have an answer at graduation — not a lecture, just a different way to look at the same question
variations: You don't have to have the next step figured out. Sometimes you have to start moving before the answer can catch up with you.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自台灣畢業祝賀語合集，改寫為更有溫度的版本

zh-tw.content:
你走進來的時候，什麼都不確定。

你走出去的時候，大概還是什麼都不確定。

但有一件事不一樣了——
你知道自己撐得過去。

這是這幾年給你最值錢的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那些還在懷疑自己準備好了沒的畢業生——這句話不給建議，只是提醒他們已經做到了什麼
variations: 學歷不是畢業給你的最重要的東西。那個「我曾經以為不行，但還是撐過去了」的記憶，才是。

en.content:
You walked in uncertain about everything.

You're walking out still uncertain about most things.

But one thing has changed —
you now know you can make it through.

That's the most valuable thing these years gave you.
en.editorNote: For graduates who still doubt whether they're ready — this doesn't give advice, it just reminds them what they've already proven
variations: The most important thing graduation gave you isn't the degree. It's the memory of thinking you couldn't — and doing it anyway.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 TinTint 畢業金句文章，改寫為更口語自然的台灣用語

zh-tw.content:
有一種人，
你們不會每天說話，
不會固定約出來，
甚至有時候幾個月都不聯絡。

但只要其中一個人說：「最近怎樣？」
就好像沒有消失過一樣。

願你畢業之後，身邊還有這種人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你不常聯絡但每次聯絡都像昨天才見面的人——有時候說出來比什麼都更重要
variations: 不是每一段友情都需要每天確認。真正深的那種，沉默幾個月再回來，還是同一個頻率。

en.content:
There's a kind of person
you don't talk to every day,
never have a standing date with,
sometimes go months without a word.

But the moment one of you says, 'Hey, how have you been?'
it's like no time passed at all.

Hope you still have someone like that after graduation.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend you don't talk to constantly but who always picks up right where you left off — saying it out loud sometimes matters more than anything
variations: Not every friendship needs daily check-ins. The real ones go quiet for months and come back on exactly the same frequency.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Shikshanation 畢業語錄合集，改寫為搞笑現實風格

zh-tw.content:
歡迎來到真實世界。

這裡沒有學期。
沒有寒暑假。
考試沒有固定時間，
而且大部分都沒有標準答案。

別擔心——
其實也沒有人知道這裡的規則，
大家都是邊玩邊學的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業派對上說這段，或是傳給剛進社會的朋友——第一句是威嚇，後面幾句才是真正的安慰
variations: 真實世界最大的祕密是：大部分的大人也不確定自己在做什麼，只是裝出來的比你熟練一點。

en.content:
Welcome to the real world.

No semesters here.
No summer break.
The tests come without warning
and most of them don't have a correct answer.

Don't worry though —
nobody actually knows the rules.
Everyone is making it up as they go.
en.editorNote: Say this at a graduation party or send it to a friend entering the workforce — the opening line is a scare, the rest is the real comfort
variations: The biggest secret about the real world: most adults are just as uncertain as you are. They're just better at looking like they aren't.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 2026 IG 畢業 caption 趨勢，改寫為原創口語版本

zh-tw.content:
劇情大轉折：我真的畢業了。

學位到手。
人生載入中……

（附帶說明：徵才請洽本人。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 三段式IG caption，搭配學士帽照片效果超好——從驚喜到宣告到搞笑求職，一鏡到底
variations: 學位：已解鎖。接下來要解鎖什麼，等我想到再說。

en.content:
Plot twist: I actually graduated.

Degree: secured.
Life: loading…

(P.S. — please hire me.)
en.editorNote: Three-beat IG caption that pairs perfectly with a cap-toss photo — surprise, announcement, and a self-aware job-search punchline all in one
variations: Degree: unlocked. Whatever comes next: TBD. But I graduated, so we're off to a solid start.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Canvas Discount 畢業語錄，改寫為台灣口語感人版本

zh-tw.content:
這張畢業證書上只有我的名字，
但你們都知道這不是只有我一個人的事。

謝謝你們每一次沒問我為什麼回來這麼晚，
每一次假裝沒看到我眼睛紅紅的，
每一次不問直接去幫我煮了一碗東西。

這張紙，也有你們的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業典禮上傳給家人，或是貼到IG tag父母——比「感謝爸媽」四個字更有份量，也更讓人鼻酸
variations: 有些人從頭到尾沒有出現在任何一張課堂照片裡，但如果沒有他們，我不會走到這一步。這句話，是給他們的。

en.content:
Only my name is on this diploma.
But you all know this wasn't just me.

Thank you for every time you didn't ask why I came home so late.
For every time you pretended not to notice my eyes were red.
For every time you just went ahead and made me something to eat without saying a word.

This piece of paper belongs to you too.
en.editorNote: Send to family at graduation or post and tag your parents — more weight than just 'thanks mom and dad,' and more likely to make someone tear up
variations: Some people never showed up in a single class photo, but without them I wouldn't have made it here. This one's for them.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Career Nuts 搞笑畢業語錄，改寫為台灣廢話文學風格

zh-tw.content:
我花了好幾年的學費，
換來一張紙、一頂帽子、
還有「知道自己不知道什麼」的能力。

這最後一樣，
其實是最值錢的。

（但我還是希望那頂帽子可以留著。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 這句話在典禮上說是笑點，但說完之後大家都會想一下——括號那句是給需要笑點收尾的人
variations: 大學四年教會我最重要的事，不是任何一門課的內容，而是「找到答案的方法」。還有怎麼在最後一天交報告。

en.content:
I traded years of tuition for a piece of paper, a hat,
and the ability to know what I don't know.

That last one is actually the most valuable thing I got.

(I'm still glad I get to keep the hat though.)
en.editorNote: Gets a laugh at the ceremony but leaves everyone thinking for a second — the parenthetical is for anyone who needs a lighter landing
variations: The most important thing college taught me wasn't in any syllabus. It was how to find the answer — and how to submit it the morning it's due.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Pinkoi 畢業祝福語合集，改寫為台灣年輕人口吻的勵志語錄

zh-tw.content:
有一件事沒有人告訴你：

畢業之後，
你終於可以自己選要成為什麼樣的人，
不再只是「某某學校的學生」。

這個自由有時候讓人害怕，
但它也是你等了好幾年的東西。

好好用它。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些對「不再是學生」這件事感到茫然的人——這句話不是告訴你要做什麼，而是提醒你現在的選擇是你自己的
variations: 畢業不是終點，是你第一次真正可以為自己做決定的起點。慢慢來，但記得出發。

en.content:
Here's something nobody tells you:

After graduation,
you finally get to decide who you want to become —
not just 'a student at somewhere.'

That freedom can feel scary.
But it's also what you've been waiting for.

Make the most of it.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels unmoored now that 'being a student' is no longer their identity — this isn't telling you what to do, it's reminding you the choice is now yours
variations: Graduation isn't an ending. It's the first moment you get to make decisions that are truly your own. Take your time, but don't forget to start.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Minted 畢業語錄集，改寫為台灣口語感人告別語錄

zh-tw.content:
你會記得的，不是考了幾分，
也不是交了幾份報告。

是那個下雨天一起躲在走廊等放晴的下午。
是那個考完試坐在台階上什麼都不說的沉默。
是那個不知道為什麼突然就笑起來的瞬間。

這些，才是你帶走的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天傳給一起走過來的人，或是配上老照片貼到限時動態——這種細節感的文字比任何名言都更容易讓人紅眼眶
variations: 多年後你回想這段時光，記住的不會是那些大事，而是那些小到說不出口、卻剛好被你留下來的片刻。

en.content:
What you'll remember isn't your GPA
or how many papers you turned in.

It's the rainy afternoon you and a friend got stuck under the same overhang waiting for it to clear.
The silence after a hard exam, sitting on the steps, no words needed.
The moment something suddenly became funny for no reason at all.

Those are the things you're actually taking with you.
en.editorNote: Send on graduation day to the people who shared those quiet moments with you, or pair with an old photo for a story post — this kind of specific detail hits harder than any famous quote
variations: Years from now, the big events will blur. What stays are the small moments you didn't even know you were saving.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「畢業像軟體更新」笑話，改寫為台灣口語廢話文學版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業就像手機系統更新。

你知道遲早要來，
你也知道更新完會變更好，
但按下去的那一瞬間你還是緊張。

而且更新途中絕對不能關機。

——你現在就在更新中，請耐心等待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很適合在畢業典禮或謝師宴上說，科技世代最懂這個比喻——最後一句讓人覺得「被理解了」的效果很強
variations: 畢業 = 強制更新。不管你準不準備好，進度條都會繼續跑。

en.content:
Graduating is like a system update on your phone.

You knew it was coming.
You know it's supposed to make things better.
But when the moment actually arrives, you're still a little nervous.

And whatever you do, don't shut down mid-update.

— You are currently updating. Please be patient.
en.editorNote: Works perfectly at a graduation party or farewell dinner — every tech-era grad instantly gets this metaphor, and the final line lands with a satisfying 'someone gets me' feeling
variations: Graduation = forced update. Ready or not, the progress bar keeps moving.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「感謝Google幫我撐過來」系列搞笑語錄，改寫為台灣版本（與gq-016不同角度，聚焦咖啡因與熬夜）

zh-tw.content:
今天站在這裡，我要感謝許多人。

感謝咖啡，讓我在最後一堂課沒有睡著。
感謝便利商店，在凌晨三點還開著門迎接我。
感謝那個不知道幾點的自己，決定再撐一下。

你們都是這張畢業證書的共同作者。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在謝師宴或畢業派對上說，有笑點也有暖點，台灣便利商店文化讓這段在本地特別有共鳴
variations: 畢業感言誠實版：謝謝咖啡、謝謝7-11、謝謝那個凌晨五點還在改報告的我。

en.content:
Today, standing here, I want to thank a few people.

Coffee, for keeping me awake through the final lecture.
The convenience store, for being open at 3 a.m. when I needed it most.
And the version of me who decided, in the dark, to keep going a little longer.

You are all co-authors of this diploma.
en.editorNote: Say this at a graduation dinner or send as a toast — gets a laugh and a nod, the 'keep going a little longer' line lands with genuine warmth
variations: Honest graduation speech: thank you to coffee, late-night snacks, and the 2 a.m. version of me who somehow believed it was going to be fine.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自2026年畢業IG caption趨勢中的Taylor Swift era風格，改寫為台灣口語版本（與gq-004 era主題不同，聚焦歌詞感）

zh-tw.content:
我把最好的年紀都給了這裡。

還有最慘的期末考、最睏的上課時光、
最多報告的學期、還有那幾頓沒吃的午餐。

但如果可以重來，
我還是選這裡。

（可能啦。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配上四年前的新生照對比畢業照——括號那句是給不想太肉麻的人準備的逃生出口，整體走懷念又帶一點幽默的路線
variations: 這裡拿走了我很多東西，但它也給了我一些永遠帶走的。我們扯平了。

en.content:
I gave some of my best years to this place.

Also my worst finals, my most sleep-deprived mornings,
my most deadline-heavy semesters, and several lunches I just skipped.

But if I could do it over,
I'd still choose here.

(Probably.)
en.editorNote: Pair with a side-by-side of your freshman and graduation photos — the parenthetical is the exit ramp for anyone who doesn't want to sound too sentimental, but the whole thing lands as nostalgic with a wink
variations: This place took a lot from me. But it gave me things I'll carry forever. I'd call it even.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「找不到工作可以去念研究所」幽默語錄，改寫為台灣口語搞笑版本

zh-tw.content:
找不到工作怎麼辦？

去念研究所。

念完研究所還是找不到工作怎麼辦？

去念博士。

念完博士還是找不到工作怎麼辦？

恭喜你，你可以去當教授了。

人生總是有出路的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在謝師宴或畢業派對上說最好，引爆全場然後讓大家笑著想自己的下一步——最後一句是正面收尾
variations: 有一種人生規劃叫做：先畢業，再說。這也是一種計畫，而且非常多人在用。

en.content:
Can't find a job after graduation?

Go get a Master's.

Still can't find a job after that?

Go get a PhD.

Still nothing?

Congratulations — you're now qualified to become a professor.

There's always a way forward.
en.editorNote: Best delivered out loud at a graduation party — triggers a big laugh and then a moment of 'wait, is that actually my plan?' for half the room; the final line gives it an optimistic landing
variations: One valid life plan: graduate first, figure out the rest later. It's a strategy. A lot of people are using it.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自台灣2026可愛畢業祝福語合集，改寫為有溫度的勵志短語

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，你不會消失。

你只是移動到了另一個地方，
繼續閃著你的光。

不管在哪裡，記得讓人找得到你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給即將各奔東西的朋友，或是貼在限時動態tag所有人——簡短有力，比長篇大論更容易讓人記住
variations: 我們不是散了，只是換了地方繼續發光。記得偶爾讓我看到你在哪裡。

en.content:
After graduation, you don't disappear.

You just move to a different place
and keep shining from there.

Wherever you end up — stay findable.
en.editorNote: Send to friends scattering in different directions, or post a story and tag everyone — short and direct, the kind of thing people actually remember
variations: We're not gone. We're just shining from different spots now. Make sure I can still find yours.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自2026 IG畢業caption趨勢中「Degree: complete. Plan: unclear.」系列，改寫為台灣口語版本

zh-tw.content:
學位：拿到了。
計畫：稍後再說。
錢包：在哭。
臉：無所謂。

——這是我目前全部的畢業報告。
zh-tw.editorNote: 四行條列式IG caption，節奏俐落，貼上去直接就是一篇，適合不想想太多但還是要發文的人
variations: 學位到手，其他的細節我們以後再討論。

en.content:
Degree: secured.
Plan: TBD.
Wallet: crying.
Face: unbothered.

— This is my complete graduation report.
en.editorNote: Four-line list-format IG caption with a punchy closing line — drop it straight into the caption box and you're done, great for people who want to post without overthinking it
variations: Degree: done. Everything else: details to be worked out later.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自Personal Chic搞笑畢業語錄中學費與薪水的幽默對比，改寫為台灣語境版本

zh-tw.content:
辛辛苦苦念了好幾年，
領到一張紙。

然後去找一份工作，
發現這份工作不需要這張紙，
但需要「兩年相關工作經驗」。

恭喜你正式踏入社會。
這裡歡迎你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在謝師宴或朋友間說，讓大家苦笑一下——最後那句「這裡歡迎你」是諷刺也是真心，效果很微妙
variations: 大學四年的精華，有時候在求職網站上被濃縮成「不符合基本資格」五個字。繼續加油。

en.content:
Years of hard work.
One piece of paper.

Then you go look for a job
and discover the job doesn't require the paper —
but does require 'two years of relevant experience.'

Welcome to the workforce.
We've been expecting you.
en.editorNote: Say this at a graduation dinner to get a collective groan-laugh — the final line reads as sarcasm and warmth at the same time, which is the whole point
variations: Sometimes four years of college gets reduced to 'does not meet minimum qualifications' on a job listing. Keep going anyway.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自Today.com畢業語錄中感謝師長的主題，改寫為台灣口語感人版本

zh-tw.content:
有些老師，
你當時沒說謝謝，
因為你以為以後還有機會。

畢業之後你才發現，
那個「以後」其實就是現在。

如果你心裡有這樣一個人，
今天是個好時機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業前後傳給那位你一直想說謝謝、卻還沒開口的老師——不一定要長篇大論，光是傳這段話過去就夠了
variations: 有一種謝謝，說晚了不代表不算，只是說的時候會更有份量。今天說，剛剛好。

en.content:
There are teachers you never thanked,
because you assumed there would be more time.

After graduation, you realize
that 'more time' is actually right now.

If someone like that comes to mind,
today is a good day to say something.
en.editorNote: Send before or after graduation to a teacher you always meant to thank but never did — you don't need a long message; forwarding this alone is enough of a start
variations: A thank-you said late still counts. It just lands with a little more weight. Today is a good time.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「entering adult tutorial mode」遊戲化畢業語錄，改寫為台灣遊戲世代口吻

zh-tw.content:
恭喜你完成新手教學關卡。

現在進入主線任務：
找工作、繳房租、學會自己煮飯、
還有假裝你知道報稅是什麼。

難度：地獄。
存檔點：無。

祝你好運。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給遊戲世代的畢業生，用闖關邏輯解釋「踏入社會」這件事——既是玩笑又是事實，在謝師宴或IG上說都很有梗
variations: 學生時代是遊戲的教學關卡。現在你要打的，才是真正的遊戲。好消息是：你已經知道怎麼按按鈕了。

en.content:
Congratulations on completing the tutorial.

You are now entering the main campaign:
Find a job. Pay rent. Learn to cook.
Pretend you understand how taxes work.

Difficulty: nightmare mode.
Checkpoints: none.

Good luck out there.
en.editorNote: The gaming-generation take on entering adulthood — works as an IG caption or a toast at a graduation party, especially funny because every single part of it is also completely true
variations: School was the tutorial level. Now you're in the actual game. The good news: you already know how to press the buttons.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「畢業後那個從不沉默的群組突然安靜」這個普遍現象，改寫為台灣口語版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，那個群組會慢慢安靜下來。

從每天幾百則訊息，
到偶爾一則「大家最近好嗎」，
再到沒有人回應。

但群組還在。
沒有人退出。

這就是它現在存在的方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳到那個你們班或社團的群組裡，或是貼在限時動態——不煽情，只是說出了一件大家都知道但沒人先說的事
variations: 群組沒有解散，只是大家都在用沉默的方式繼續留在裡面。這也是一種在一起。

en.content:
After graduation, the group chat slowly goes quiet.

From hundreds of messages a day
to an occasional 'hey, how's everyone doing'
to no reply at all.

But the chat is still there.
Nobody left.

That's how it stays together now.
en.editorNote: Drop this in your class or club group chat, or post it to your stories — not sentimental, just honest about something everyone already knows but nobody says first
variations: The group chat never disbanded. Everyone's just staying in it quietly. That's still a kind of together.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自畢業典禮演講搞笑語錄中關於「個人主義」的幽默，改寫為台灣口語廢話文學版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業典禮上，他們叫幾千個穿著一模一樣的人，
乖乖坐成一排，
然後告訴你：

「要做自己，勇於與眾不同。」

我覺得這很好笑。
但我還是鼓掌了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 這句話說出來全場都會笑，因為每個人都有這個體驗——可以在典禮後傳給同學，或是貼到限時動態讓大家秒懂
variations: 人生建議：先把帽子穿得跟大家一樣，再去談「你有多與眾不同」。這樣比較有說服力。

en.content:
At graduation, they put thousands of people in identical outfits,
line them up in neat rows,
then tell you:

'Be yourself. Dare to be different.'

I thought that was pretty funny.
But I clapped anyway.
en.editorNote: Say this out loud and watch half the room laugh immediately — everyone has sat through this exact speech; works as a caption on a crowd photo from the ceremony
variations: Pro tip: put on the exact same hat as ten thousand other people, then deliver your speech about standing out from the crowd. Very convincing.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「具體細節比通用感謝更有力」的caption建議，改寫為台灣口語版本，聚焦那些不在畢業照裡卻最重要的人

zh-tw.content:
有人問我：「畢業後最想念的是什麼？」

不是課，不是社團活動，
不是什麼重要的大事。

是那個在圖書館門口等我、
什麼都不說只是遞給我一杯飲料的下午。

就那一個下午。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個做過這件事的人——他可能不記得了，但你記得，這才是重點。配上當時的照片效果加倍
variations: 最難忘的不是大事，往往是那個時間點、那個人、那個剛好的溫度，恰好被你記住了。

en.content:
Someone asked me: 'What will you miss most after graduation?'

Not a class. Not a club event.
Not anything significant.

Just one afternoon — someone waiting at the library door,
saying nothing, just handing me a drink.

Just that one afternoon.
en.editorNote: Send to whoever that person was — they may not even remember it, but you do, and that's the whole point; pairs perfectly with a photo from that era
variations: The most unforgettable moments are rarely the big ones. They're the specific person, specific moment, specific warmth that just happened to stick.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「畢業袍像高級垃圾袋」的幽默，改寫為台灣口語搞笑版本，聚焦學士帽學士袍的視覺荒謬感

zh-tw.content:
學士袍是人類史上最公平的服裝。

不管你睡了幾堂課、熬了幾個通宵、
拿了幾個不及格、交了幾份遲到的報告——

穿上去，大家都一樣好看。

這才是真正的學術精神。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在典禮當天傳給同學，或是配上一張正式的學士袍合照貼上去——用來打破典禮的莊嚴感效果一流
variations: 學士袍：均一化一切差異的神奇布料。四年的高下，穿上去之後看起來都是同一個人。

en.content:
The graduation gown is the most democratic garment ever made.

No matter how many classes you slept through,
how many all-nighters you pulled,
how many Cs you collected,
or how many late submissions you filed —

put it on, and everyone looks exactly the same.

Now that's the true spirit of academia.
en.editorNote: Send to classmates on ceremony day or pair with a formal group photo — perfect for cutting through the solemnity of the occasion with one well-timed observation
variations: The graduation gown: one garment to erase four years of academic inequality. Under the robe, we are all the same person.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「to everyone who believed in me」感謝系列caption，改寫為台灣口語版本，角度轉為感謝那些曾經質疑你的人

zh-tw.content:
謝謝相信我的人——你們是我的動力。

也謝謝不相信我的人——
你們也是我的動力。

說真的，動力越多越好。

（都謝謝。）
zh-tw.editorNote: IG caption反轉梗，幽默但有力，一句話讓人又笑又覺得被激勵——特別適合那些曾經被看輕過的人發這篇
variations: 有一種感謝叫做：謝謝你當初說我不行，因為我把它存起來了，然後今天用掉了。

en.content:
Thank you to everyone who believed in me — you were my motivation.

And thank you to everyone who didn't —
you were also my motivation.

Honestly, the more the better.

(Appreciated all of you.)
en.editorNote: The classic reversal format — funny and punchy but genuinely empowering; especially satisfying for anyone who was ever doubted and wants to say something about it without being too serious
variations: There's a kind of gratitude that sounds like: thank you for telling me I couldn't. I saved it. I used it today.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「你擁有大腦就能主導方向」（Dr. Seuss）系列鼓勵語，改寫為台灣口語版本，聚焦「不確定本身也是一種可能性」

zh-tw.content:
每個人在畢業的時候，
都假裝自己知道接下來要幹嘛。

大部分的人其實不知道。

這不是問題。

「不確定」只是代表你的可能性還沒被決定。
而那，才是畢業最好的狀態。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給所有覺得自己「還沒準備好」的畢業生——這句話不是要你放棄計畫，而是讓你知道不確定不等於輸在起跑點
variations: 沒有計畫，不代表沒有未來。只代表你的未來，還沒有被限縮成某一種形狀。

en.content:
At graduation, everyone pretends they know what comes next.

Most people don't.

That's not a problem.

'Uncertain' just means your possibilities haven't been decided yet.
And that, honestly, is the best state to graduate in.
en.editorNote: For every graduate who feels underprepared — this isn't telling you to abandon planning, it's reframing uncertainty as open possibility rather than failure
variations: No plan doesn't mean no future. It means your future hasn't been narrowed down to one shape yet.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「Graduated… still buffering」系列搞笑caption，改寫為台灣廢話文學風格

zh-tw.content:
畢業了。

系統正在載入人生下一頁。

請稍候——

（轉圈圈中）
zh-tw.editorNote: 最短的畢業IG caption之一，配上學士帽照片直接發，不需要解釋，人人都看懂那個「轉圈圈」是什麼感覺
variations: 學位：已取得。人生：載入中……進度條卡在37%，目前正在重試。

en.content:
Graduated.

Loading next chapter of life.

Please wait —

(buffering)
en.editorNote: One of the shortest graduation IG captions that still says everything — pair with a cap photo and post directly, no explanation needed; anyone who's ever stared at a spinning wheel gets it instantly
variations: Degree: obtained. Life: loading… progress bar stuck at 37%, currently retrying.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「too educated to be this broke」系列搞笑語錄，改寫為台灣口語版本，聚焦學貸與薪水的反差

zh-tw.content:
我花了好幾年，
換來一張有我名字的紙，
還有一筆也有我名字的債。

至少兩樣都是我的。

這叫做「擁有感」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 說給那些剛畢業、口袋空空、貸款催繳通知也到了的人——笑點在最後一句，現實感讓人苦笑後又有點釋然
variations: 畢業禮物清單：一頂帽子、一張紙、一個前途、還有一筆等著被還的錢。禮物很豐盛。

en.content:
I spent years
to get a piece of paper with my name on it —
and a debt also with my name on it.

At least both are mine.

That's what they call ownership.
en.editorNote: For the freshly graduated, broke, and already getting repayment notices — the punchline lands on 'ownership,' which gets a laugh before the quiet nod of recognition sets in
variations: Graduation gifts received: one hat, one diploma, one future, one loan repayment schedule. Quite the haul.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「I finally understand everything, just in time to leave」概念，改寫為台灣口語版本

zh-tw.content:
大三大四我終於搞懂這門課在說什麼了。

搞懂的時間點：
畢業典禮前三天。

知識，真的是一種緣分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那些「我終於看懂這個理論了」的同學，或是配上一張拍學分表的照片——反差感讓人笑，但最後一句又有點真
variations: 四年的學習，精華都集中在最後一個月。這是一種高效率，只是方向有點特別。

en.content:
In my third and fourth year, I finally started understanding what this subject was actually about.

Timing of this realization:
three days before graduation.

Knowledge really does have its own schedule.
en.editorNote: Send to classmates who had that late-click moment, or pair with a photo of your transcript — the irony lands instantly, and the final line gives it a wry philosophical spin
variations: Four years of studying, and the real understanding arrived in the last month. Technically very efficient. Directionally questionable.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自AI世代畢業生的自嘲梗，改寫為原創台灣口語版本，角度聚焦「AI幫了很多但畢業證書還是你的」

zh-tw.content:
感謝幫過我的每一個人。

教授、同學、圖書館員、
深夜還在更新的線上資料庫，
還有那個不具名的AI助理。

不管怎樣，畢業證書上是我的名字。

我們都知道這代表什麼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026年最有時代感的畢業梗——點出AI世代學生的共同經歷，最後一句留白讓人自行解讀，笑點夠但不咄咄逼人
variations: 這個世代的感謝名單比以前的人長了一欄。但成績單上的名字還是只有一個。

en.content:
Thank you to everyone who helped me along the way.

Professors, classmates, librarians,
late-night online databases,
and one unnamed AI assistant.

Either way, the diploma has my name on it.

We all know what that means.
en.editorNote: The most zeitgeist-aware graduation joke of 2026 — acknowledges what everyone already knows without being preachy about it; the final line lets people fill in their own interpretation
variations: This generation's acknowledgment list has one more line than previous generations. But there's still only one name on the degree.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「每次失敗都是通往成功的一步」系列勵志語，改寫為台灣口語版本，聚焦「失敗被重新定義為進度」

zh-tw.content:
你在這裡走錯過，失敗過，重來過。

那些不是你的汙點，
那是你的進度條。

畢業的你，
比任何時候都更知道怎麼繼續。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些走得不順、繞過路、甚至重考或延畢的畢業生——不說教，只是換一個框架看那些「不完美的紀錄」
variations: 不是每個人都直線走到畢業的。那些彎路，換個說法叫做：你比別人多看了幾條路。

en.content:
You made wrong turns here. You failed things. You started over.

Those aren't marks against you —
they're your progress bar.

The version of you graduating today
knows better than ever how to keep going.
en.editorNote: For graduates whose path wasn't straight — those who retook exams, changed majors, took longer, or hit walls — reframes every stumble as accumulated knowledge rather than a deficit
variations: Not everyone walks a straight line to graduation. Those detours? Another way to say you've seen more roads than most.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「終於不用再做組報告」系列搞笑語錄，改寫為台灣口語廢話文學版本，聚焦組報告的黑暗面

zh-tw.content:
在這裡，我學到了很多東西。

我學到了怎麼自己一個人完成「五個人的報告」。
我學到了怎麼在組員名字也掛上去之後，
還是保持微笑。
我學到了：有些人不是你的隊友，
他們只是剛好在你的隊。

這些，沒有在課綱裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個跟你一起扛完組報告的人——或是傳給那幾個完全沒出現的組員也可以，他們應該懂
variations: 最鍛鍊一個人的，不是期末考，是那份本來五個人做、最後變成一個人做的組報告。

en.content:
In this place, I learned a great deal.

I learned how to complete a 'five-person group project' entirely by myself.
I learned how to keep smiling even after everyone else's names
were listed on the cover page too.
I learned that some people aren't your teammates —
they just happen to be in your group.

None of that was in the syllabus.
en.editorNote: Send to whoever actually did the work with you — or forward to the group members who vanished, they'll understand
variations: The greatest test of character isn't the final exam. It's the group project that quietly became a solo project somewhere around week two.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「Diploma earned, sleep still pending」搞笑語錄，改寫為台灣口語版本，聚焦睡眠時差與畢業之間的荒謬落差

zh-tw.content:
學位：拿到了。
睡眠：還在補。

我把該給的都給了這四年，
包括無數個凌晨三點。

現在只求一件事：
睡一覺，醒來再開始新人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些熬了無數個夜才走到今天的畢業生，也是最誠實的畢業感言格式之一——短、有共鳴、貼上IG直接就是一篇
variations: 畢業典禮結束後最想做的事第一名：睡覺。這不是沒出息，這是正常人。

en.content:
Degree: secured.
Sleep: still owed.

I gave this place everything across four years —
including a long list of 3 a.m. nights.

Now I want just one thing:
a solid sleep, and then I'll start the rest of my life.
en.editorNote: For every graduate who ran on empty to get here — also works perfectly as an IG caption right after the ceremony when you're exhausted and relieved at the same time
variations: The first thing on my post-graduation to-do list: sleep. This is not a lack of ambition. This is basic biology.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「adulting unlocked, send help」系列搞笑梗，改寫為台灣口語廢話文學版本，聚焦成人生活技能突然需要被解鎖

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，你會突然需要知道很多事情。

保險怎麼保。
稅怎麼報。
健保卡要放在哪裡。
冰箱裡的東西放多久算過期。

沒有人在學校教過你這些。

這是一種很特別的畢業禮物，
叫做「自己查」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在謝師宴或畢業派對上說這段，大家都會笑著點頭——可以直接拿去當致詞，幽默但有真實感
variations: 成人的第一關：發現學校沒教你任何你真正需要用的東西。第二關：Google它。

en.content:
After graduation, you'll suddenly need to know a lot of things.

How insurance works.
How to file taxes.
Where to keep your health card.
How long leftovers can actually stay in the fridge.

Nobody taught you any of this.

This is a very special graduation gift.
It's called: figure it out yourself.
en.editorNote: Read this at a graduation dinner or send it to your class group — everyone laughs and nods because it's all completely true; works as a toast opener too
variations: Level one of adulthood: realizing school didn't teach you anything you'll actually need. Level two: Googling everything.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「畢業照 vs. 現實」系列搞笑語錄，改寫為台灣口語版本，聚焦典禮當天外表莊嚴但內心一片混亂的反差

zh-tw.content:
畢業照裡的我：
莊重、自信、面帶微笑，
看起來一切都在掌握之中。

畢業照拍完之後的我：
「好餓。」
「那個典禮什麼時候結束？」
「我的帽子快掉了。」

還好相機只拍一瞬間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 配上正式的畢業照發IG，把這段當caption——反差笑點強，而且每個人都能對號入座
variations: 畢業照是人生最接近「假裝自己準備好了」的那一秒。拍完之後大家又都回去茫然了。

en.content:
Me in my graduation photo:
poised, confident, smiling,
looking like I have it all together.

Me two minutes after the graduation photo:
'I'm starving.'
'When does this ceremony actually end?'
'My cap is about to fall off.'

Lucky for me, cameras only capture one second.
en.editorNote: Post this alongside your official graduation photo — the contrast between the caption and the polished picture is the entire joke, and everyone in that crowd had the same internal monologue
variations: A graduation photo is the one second of your life when you convincingly looked like you had everything figured out. The rest of the day was the usual chaos.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「你已經改變了，只是自己沒察覺」的畢業演講主題，改寫為台灣口語感人版本，聚焦成長的無聲發生

zh-tw.content:
你沒有注意到自己是什麼時候變的。

不是某個大事之後，
也不是某個特定的早晨。

就是某一天，你回頭看，
才發現當初那個走進來的你，
跟現在走出去的你，
已經不是同一個人了。

這就是這幾年最安靜、也最真實的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天發，或是配上入學第一天的照片——不需要過度解釋，這幾句話本身就夠了
variations: 成長不會在某個瞬間轟然發生。它是那些你以為沒意義的日子，慢慢疊出來的。

en.content:
You didn't notice when you started to change.

It wasn't after some defining moment.
It wasn't on any particular morning.

It's just that one day, you looked back,
and the person who walked in on day one
and the person walking out today —
they're not the same anymore.

That's the quietest and most real thing these years did.
en.editorNote: Post on graduation day or pair with a photo from your very first day — no over-explanation needed, these lines carry their own weight
variations: Growth doesn't arrive with a big announcement. It's built slowly from all the days you thought didn't count.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自2026流行的「In my graduated era」與「Degree secured」短梗，改寫為台灣口語廢話文學版本，聚焦畢業即「正式失業」的反差自嘲

zh-tw.content:
更新一下我的職業欄：

從「學生」
變成「待業中」。

聽起來像降級，
其實只是換一個更誠實的講法。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業後一兩週還在投履歷的人發IG，自嘲但不悲情，按讚率特別高
variations: 畢業證書到手的那一刻，我從一個有身份的人，變成一個有待業時間的人。
畢業不是失業，畢業只是「正在思考人生方向」的浪漫說法。

en.content:
Quick update on my job title:

From 'student'
to 'currently exploring opportunities.'

Sounds like a downgrade.
It's really just a more honest version of the same thing.
en.editorNote: Perfect for the post-graduation week when you're still applying to jobs — self-aware without being a downer, gets the most engagement
variations: The moment I got my diploma, I went from a person with a status to a person with availability.
I'm not unemployed. I'm just romantically figuring out my life direction.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「we made it out of the group chat era」流行梗，改寫為台灣口語感人版本，聚焦群組從熱鬧到安靜的真實演變

zh-tw.content:
我們的群組以前一天可以跳兩百多則。

誰要訂飲料、誰先到教室、
下課要不要去吃火鍋、
誰昨天又被當了。

現在偶爾跳一則，
是有人結婚，
或者有人換工作。

沒有人不見，
只是大家都長大了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業幾年後再回頭看那個群組會更有感，適合畢業典禮當天或週年回憶時發
variations: 群組沒有解散，只是訊息變慢了——這是長大最安靜的證據。

en.content:
Our group chat used to hit 200 messages a day.

Who's ordering drinks, who got to class first,
are we doing hotpot after,
who just failed another midterm.

Now it lights up once in a while —
someone's getting married,
someone changed jobs.

Nobody disappeared.
We just grew up.
en.editorNote: Lands harder a few years after graduation when you scroll back through that chat — works for graduation day or for an anniversary throwback post
variations: The group chat didn't end. The messages just slowed down — and that's the quietest proof we grew up.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「somehow convinced everyone I knew what I was doing for four years」搞笑系列，改寫為台灣口語版本，聚焦學生時代的全員裝懂文化

zh-tw.content:
我成功讓學校相信我是來唸書的。

我成功讓老師相信我有讀書。
我成功讓同學相信我聽得懂。
我成功讓自己相信我會畢業。

結果——我真的畢業了。

人類最強大的能力，
是互相裝懂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 謝師宴上講這段穩穩接住全場的笑點，也適合貼IG配上畢業照，自嘲又有共鳴
variations: 從入學到畢業，我都靠一招撐過來：點頭、微笑、回家再Google。

en.content:
I successfully convinced the school I came here to study.

I convinced my professors I'd done the reading.
I convinced my classmates I followed the lecture.
I convinced myself I'd actually graduate.

And somehow — I really did.

Humanity's strongest skill
is collectively pretending to understand.
en.editorNote: Crushes at a graduation dinner toast or as an Instagram caption — self-deprecating, instantly relatable, everyone's been there
variations: From day one to graduation, my entire strategy was: nod, smile, Google it later.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自畢業前寫給同學的真誠信件主題，改寫為台灣口語感人版本，聚焦那些沒說出口的感謝

zh-tw.content:
有些謝謝，我一直沒有說出口。

謝謝那個借我筆記的人。
謝謝那個在我崩潰的時候沒問為什麼，只說「先吃飯」的人。
謝謝那個願意聽我講同一件事三次的人。
謝謝那個讓我覺得，這幾年沒有那麼難熬的人。

如果你看到這段，
你應該會知道我在說你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業前發給特定的朋友，比直接說「謝謝你」更打動人——也很適合放在畢業相簿的最後一頁
variations: 謝謝你曾經出現在那段時間裡，讓那段時間，變成我願意一直記得的時間。

en.content:
There are some thank-yous I never said out loud.

Thank you for letting me copy your notes.
Thank you for not asking what was wrong — just saying 'eat first.'
Thank you for listening to the same story three times.
Thank you for being the reason these years didn't feel so heavy.

If you're reading this,
you probably already know I'm talking about you.
en.editorNote: Send privately to the specific friend before graduation — more moving than a direct 'thank you' and a perfect closing page for a graduation photo album
variations: Thank you for showing up during that stretch of my life and turning it into a stretch of my life I actually want to remember.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「The tassel was worth the hassle」與「I worked my tassel off」系列搞笑caption，改寫為台灣口語版本，聚焦熬夜趕作業的學生記憶

zh-tw.content:
畢業證書到手的那一刻，
我才終於可以對著我的黑眼圈說：

「謝謝你陪我撐到最後。」

這張紙不便宜——
它的單位不是學費，
是那些睡掉的早餐、
喝掉的咖啡、
跟我自己交換過好幾次的睡眠時數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 拿著畢業證書的IG照超適合配這段，自帶笑點又戳中所有熬過due的學生——特別是2026剛畢業的那批
variations: 畢業不是領一張紙，是把你過去四年欠睡眠的帳，象徵性地結清。

en.content:
The moment I held that diploma,
I finally got to look at my eye bags and say:

"Thanks for sticking with me."

This piece of paper wasn't cheap —
the currency wasn't tuition.
It was skipped breakfasts,
too much coffee,
and all the sleep I traded with myself, over and over.
en.editorNote: Pair this with a diploma photo on Instagram — universally relatable for anyone who survived deadline season, especially the class of 2026
variations: Graduation isn't getting a piece of paper. It's symbolically closing the tab on four years of sleep debt.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「Commencement means beginning」與「This is not the finish line, it is the starting block」勵志主題，改寫為台灣口語感人版本，聚焦從終點看見起點的視角轉換

zh-tw.content:
畢業典禮的英文叫 commencement，
意思是：開始。

我以前一直覺得這個字翻錯了，
明明是結束，怎麼會是開始？

直到我走出禮堂的那一刻才懂——
結束的，是學校會幫你規劃的那條路。
開始的，是這次沒有人幫你寫課表的那條路。

比較難，也比較是你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮後寫在限動或謝師宴上講都很有重量——把英文字面拆解開來解釋，比直接說「加油」更有層次
variations: 畢業不是抵達，是換一張地圖——上面的路，要你自己畫。

en.content:
In English, graduation is called commencement.
It means: a beginning.

I used to think it was a translation mistake.
How can an ending be called a beginning?

Then I walked out of the ceremony and finally got it —
What ended was the path the school mapped out for you.
What begins is the one nobody's drawing for you anymore.

Harder. But finally, actually yours.
en.editorNote: Powerful as a post-ceremony Instagram caption or a graduation toast — unpacking the word itself lands deeper than a generic 'good luck'
variations: Graduation isn't an arrival. It's getting handed a blank map — and being told the roads are yours to draw.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自馬克吐溫「Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things you didn't do」名句改寫，聚焦畢業後敢做夢的那種勇氣，台灣口語化

zh-tw.content:
二十年後你會發現，
讓你後悔的，幾乎都不是你做過的事。

是那些你想做、但沒做的事。
是那個你想說、但沒說的人。
是那條你看了很久、但沒走過去的路。

所以從今天開始，
少想一點「我會不會後悔」，
多做一點「我以後會慶幸我有做」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業卡片、謝師宴致詞，或送給正在猶豫要不要追夢的朋友——把「後悔」翻面成「慶幸」是這段最有力的轉折
variations: 你二十年後最遺憾的，不會是你跌的那幾跤，是你站在原地沒跨出去的那幾步。

en.content:
Twenty years from now, you'll realize
almost nothing you regret is something you did.

It's the thing you wanted to do but didn't.
The person you wanted to say it to but didn't.
The road you looked at for a long time and never walked down.

So from today on,
worry less about "will I regret this?"
and more about "will I be glad I did this?"
en.editorNote: Lands in graduation cards, commencement toasts, or messages to a friend hesitating on a big move — flipping 'regret' into 'gladness' is the line that does the work
variations: Twenty years from now, your biggest regrets won't be the times you fell. They'll be the steps you stood there and never took.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「I came. I saw. I procrastinated. But I graduated.」搞笑caption系列，改寫為台灣學生超有共鳴的拖延文化版本

zh-tw.content:
我來了。
我看了。
我拖到最後一天才寫。

但我畢業了。

這證明一件事：
截止日期，
才是這世界上最強的動力來源。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照配這段IG超對味，所有有過拖延症的同學都會默默按讚——尤其是那種一打開電腦就想先看一下手機的人
variations: 畢業告訴我們一個真理：你以為你撐不過去的，最後都靠 deadline 幫你撐過去了。

en.content:
I came.
I saw.
I started it the night before.

And I graduated.

Which proves one thing:
deadlines
are the most powerful force in the universe.
en.editorNote: Tailor-made for an Instagram graduation post — every chronic procrastinator will silently double-tap, especially anyone who 'just checks their phone' before opening a laptop
variations: Graduation taught me one truth: every time you thought you couldn't pull it off, the deadline pulled you through.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自Dr. Seuss「Don't cry because it's over, smile because it happened」與告別主題改寫，聚焦那種「想哭又笑」的畢業情緒，口語化

zh-tw.content:
今天哭了一下，
但不是因為畢業很難過。

是因為想到——
那些可以隨便走進你寢室的下午，
那些一起翹課去吃午餐的中午，
那些「等我五分鐘」結果等了半小時的時刻，
以後都不會再發生了。

結束從來都不是壞事。
是太好的東西結束，
才會讓人這麼捨不得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天或畢業前一晚發限動最對味——比起「捨不得大家」這種直接的表達，把難過解構成具體畫面更打動人
variations: 畢業不是難過，是因為太多畫面太具體，具體到你一想就會鼻酸。

en.content:
Cried a little today.
Not because graduating is sad.

It's because I realized —
the afternoons you could just walk into someone's dorm,
the lunches we skipped class for,
the "give me five minutes" that always turned into thirty,
none of those are happening again.

Endings aren't the bad part.
It's that the good things ended
that makes them so hard to let go.
en.editorNote: Perfect for an Instagram story on graduation day or the night before — naming the specific scenes hits harder than a generic 'going to miss everyone'
variations: Graduation isn't sad because it ended. It's sad because the memories are specific enough that thinking about them still catches in your throat.

items.sourceNote: 受2026年「In my graduated era」風潮啟發改寫，台灣口語版

zh-tw.content:
正式進入「已畢業」時代。

症狀包含：
看到開學文會偷笑、
聽到報告就胃痛、
以及——突然發現自己已經沒有寒暑假了。

恭喜畢業，
也歡迎來到「永遠的禮拜一」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業當天發IG限動，搭配學士照或學士帽特寫，幽默又戳中所有準備進入職場的人的心聲
variations: 畢業之後才發現，原來「放假」這兩個字是有保存期限的。
從今天開始，我的人生只剩平日跟週末，沒有寒暑假這種東西了。

en.content:
Officially in my graduated era.

Symptoms include:
laughing at back-to-school posts,
stomach pain when someone says "presentation,"
and realizing —
summer break is no longer a thing.

Congrats on graduating,
and welcome to the eternal Monday.
en.editorNote: Drop this on your graduation day Instagram story with a cap-and-gown shot — funny but lands hard for anyone heading straight into the workforce
variations: Graduation taught me one thing: the words "summer break" had an expiration date all along.
From today on, my life is just weekdays and weekends. Spring break? Never heard of her.

items.sourceNote: 受“To everyone who believed in me when I didn't believe in myself”改寫，更具體更有畫面感

zh-tw.content:
這張畢業照，
其實不是只有我一個人。

是那個在我熬夜寫報告時、
半夜還幫我留一盞燈的人。
是那個聽我講同一個煩惱講第十次、
還願意說「沒事，慢慢來」的人。
是那個從來沒問過「你以後要幹嘛」、
只說「你開心就好」的人。

畢業證書上是我的名字，
但這張紙，是我們一起拿到的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮跟家人合照後發IG最催淚，把感謝寫得很具體比「謝謝爸媽」更有重量
variations: 畢業證書是一個人的名字，但走到這裡的路，從來不是一個人走的。
謝謝那些沒有逼我「成為什麼」、只希望我「過得好」的人。

en.content:
This graduation photo —
it's not really just me.

It's the person who left the kitchen light on
while I was up at 3 a.m. writing papers.
It's the person who heard the same worry from me ten times
and still said, "It's okay, take your time."
It's the person who never asked, "So what's next?"
and only said, "As long as you're happy."

My name's on the diploma,
but this piece of paper belongs to all of us.
en.editorNote: Pair with a family photo from graduation day — naming specific small moments hits way harder than a generic 'thanks mom and dad'
variations: The diploma has one name on it. But the road that got me here was never a solo trip.
Shoutout to the people who never asked who I was going to become — just hoped I'd be okay.

items.sourceNote: 受「Plot twist: I graduated」與「Chapter closed」短句潮流啟發，改寫成有層次的段落

zh-tw.content:
故事的轉折點：
我居然畢業了。

大一的時候以為自己撐不到大二，
大三的時候以為自己永遠寫不完那份報告，
但你看——
我現在站在這裡，
戴著學士帽，
手上拿著一張紙跟一個未知的未來。

下一章還沒寫，
但這一章，
結局還不錯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮現場或拍完學士照後發限動最有畫面感，「故事感」的寫法在2026年的IG上特別吃香
variations: 原來「我做不到」這四個字，每次說完還是會做到。
上一章結束。下一章空白頁。但筆已經在我手上了。

en.content:
Plot twist:
I actually graduated.

Freshman year I thought I'd never make it to sophomore year.
Junior year I thought I'd never finish that one paper.
But here I am —
cap on,
holding a piece of paper
and an entire unknown future.

The next chapter hasn't been written yet.
But this one?
Not a bad ending.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a graduation day story or post-ceremony cap shot — the 'story arc' framing is hitting hard on Instagram in 2026
variations: Turns out every time I said 'I can't do this,' I still ended up doing it.
Last chapter: closed. Next chapter: blank page. But the pen's already in my hand.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「the version of you that started here wouldn't recognize the version walking out」反思類caption改寫，聚焦自我蛻變

zh-tw.content:
新生那年走進來的我，
跟今天走出去的我，
根本不是同一個人。

那個會為了一份報告焦慮三天的我，
那個不敢舉手發言的我，
那個以為「沒朋友會死掉」的我——
他們都還在，
只是後來都被現在這個我牽著手，
一起走到這裡。

畢業不是長大，
是你終於變成那個你以前一直在等的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮前夕或拍學士照時發限動最有層次——比起「我做到了」的口號，把過去的自己具體寫出來，畫面感跟共鳴都更深
variations: 走出校門的不是同一個人，是被四年好好養大的另一個版本。
畢業最酷的不是拿到一張紙，是發現你已經不是當年那個怕東怕西的自己。

en.content:
The version of me that walked in as a freshman
and the version of me walking out today
aren't the same person.

The one who'd panic for three days over a single paper.
The one too scared to raise a hand in class.
The one who thought "having no friends means I'll die."
They're all still here —
just holding hands with who I am now,
all of us walking out together.

Graduation isn't growing up.
It's finally becoming the person you'd been waiting for the whole time.
en.editorNote: Drop on a story the night before graduation or with your cap photo — naming the old versions of yourself lands deeper than a generic 'I did it'
variations: The person walking out of these gates isn't the one who walked in. It's a version four years built from scratch.
The coolest part of graduating isn't the diploma. It's realizing you're not the scared kid who started this anymore.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「the lasts you don't notice are the ones that hurt most」概念，重寫為具體的台灣校園畫面

zh-tw.content:
你永遠不會知道，
哪一次是最後一次。

最後一次跟室友坐在地板上吃宵夜。
最後一次在圖書館閉館前衝進去借書。
最後一次在校門口那家飲料店說「老樣子」。

那些「最後一次」當下都長得很普通，
普通到你完全不會記得。

所以如果你現在還在那段日子裡——
不要等。
那個你以為還會有下一次的時刻，
可能就是了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業前夕發限動最有後座力，也很適合送給還在念書的學弟妹——把「珍惜當下」拆成具體場景，比口號有用十倍
variations: 最後一次的可怕，不是它有多重要，是它當下平凡到你連記都沒記住。
你以為還有下次的事，後來才發現那次就是最後一次。

en.content:
You'll never know
which time was the last time.

The last time you sat on the dorm floor eating midnight snacks.
The last time you sprinted into the library before it closed.
The last time you said "the usual" at the coffee place by the gate.

Every "last time" looks completely ordinary in the moment.
So ordinary you don't even remember it.

So if you're still in those days —
don't wait.
The moment you assumed there'd be a next time?
It might already be it.
en.editorNote: Posts hard the night before graduation, and lands even harder if you send it to underclassmen — breaking 'appreciate the moment' into specific scenes is ten times more effective than the slogan
variations: The scary thing about a last time isn't how big it was. It's how ordinary it felt while it was happening.
The thing you thought you'd do again — turns out, that was the last one.

items.sourceNote: 受「No cap, I graduated」「Now accepting job offers」搞笑caption啟發，改寫成台灣畢業生求職現實版

zh-tw.content:
畢業生求職階段。

第一週：「我要找有發展性的工作。」
第二週：「我要找薪水不要太低的工作。」
第三週：「我要找有冷氣的工作。」
第四週：「我要工作。」

夢想是會通膨的，
但只會往下通膨。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業後一個月發IG最有共鳴，所有正在投履歷的同學都會默默轉發——把「現實」用四週進度條呈現，比抱怨文有趣很多
variations: 求職的第一個月會教你一件事：標準這種東西，是用來下修的。
履歷投到第三十封之後你就會發現：「有興趣」這三個字其實是奢侈品。

en.content:
Post-grad job search progression.

Week 1: "I want a job with good growth potential."
Week 2: "I want a job that pays decently."
Week 3: "I want a job with air conditioning."
Week 4: "I want a job."

Dreams are subject to inflation.
But only the downward kind.
en.editorNote: Drop this a month after graduation — every classmate currently sending out resumes will quietly repost it. Showing 'reality' as a four-week progress bar lands way better than a venting post
variations: The first month of job hunting teaches you one thing: standards are made to be lowered.
By resume number thirty, you realize the word 'passionate' is a luxury item.

items.sourceNote: 受「Class of 2026 inherited a complicated world」commencement主題改寫，給台灣2026畢業生的世代訊息

zh-tw.content:
致2026畢業生：

你接到的，不是一個整理好的世界。
是一個還在裂、還在燒、還在重組的世界。

沒有人會給你一張完整的地圖，
因為地圖上的路，
很多都是我們這一屆畢業之後才開始畫的。

所以——
別等準備好。
別等清楚了。
別等大家都同意了。

你帶著的東西，剛好是這個世界缺的。
去找出那是什麼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮致詞、給學弟妹的留言，或在畢業卡片上寫——避開了「展翅高飛」這種口號，把世代焦慮翻成行動指令
variations: 你拿到的不是一個完整的世界，是一個等你補上去的世界。
畢業之後最重要的事，不是知道路在哪，是相信你走出來的就是路。

en.content:
To the Class of 2026:

What you've inherited isn't a finished world.
It's one still cracking, still burning, still rearranging itself.

No one's handing you a complete map,
because a lot of the roads on it
are ones our generation only started drawing after we graduated.

So —
don't wait until you feel ready.
don't wait until it's clear.
don't wait for everyone to agree.

What you're carrying is exactly what this world is missing.
Go figure out what that is.
en.editorNote: Works for commencement speeches, notes to underclassmen, or graduation cards — sidesteps the 'spread your wings' cliché and reframes generational anxiety as a directive
variations: You weren't handed a finished world. You were handed one waiting for you to fill in the gaps.
The most important thing after graduation isn't knowing the road. It's trusting that the road becomes a road because you walked it.

items.sourceNote: 受「the people who got me here」感謝主題啟發，改寫聚焦那些不會被點名的小角色（管理員、清潔阿姨、共筆同學）

zh-tw.content:
畢業典禮上會被謝到的，
通常是教授跟父母。

但其實還有：
那個每天清晨開教室門的警衛，
那個總是把廁所擦到發亮的阿姨，
那個考前把筆記分享給你的同學，
那個你從沒記住名字的助教，
還有那家在你深夜餓到發抖時還亮著燈的便當店。

他們不會出現在我的畢業照裡，
但這四年，
沒有他們，我根本走不到今天。

謝謝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮後或最後一次走出校門時發限動最有質感——把感謝範圍從「家人師長」延伸到那些匿名的人，會讓整則貼文的厚度跳一個級別
variations: 謝謝那些不會被合照、不會被點名、但實實在在撐起這四年日常的人。
畢業證書上沒有他們的名字，但他們也是這張紙的作者之一。

en.content:
At graduation, the people you thank out loud
are usually professors and parents.

But there's also:
the security guard who unlocked the building at 6 a.m.,
the cleaner who kept the bathrooms spotless,
the classmate who shared notes the night before the exam,
the TA whose name I never quite caught,
and the late-night takeout place that kept its light on when I was starving at 2 a.m.

None of them are in my graduation photo.
But these four years —
I don't make it without them.

Thank you.
en.editorNote: Lands beautifully after the ceremony or on your last walk out of campus — extending the thank-you list past 'family and faculty' to the unnamed people gives the post a depth that hits different
variations: Shoutout to the people who'll never be in the photo, never be named in the speech, but quietly held up four years of daily life.
Their names aren't on the diploma. But they're co-authors of it.

items.sourceNote: 受2026畢業生面對AI職場焦慮的Handshake調查啟發，把「不確定」翻成這一屆獨有的告白

zh-tw.content:
我們這一屆，
是被AI追著畢業的一屆。

念書的時候，教授說「不要用」。
找工作的時候，主管問「你會用嗎」。

沒有人教過我們這套SOP，
因為這套SOP，是我們自己一邊摔一邊寫出來的。

所以如果你現在很慌——
那是正常的。
第一批走在無人路上的人，
從來都沒有導航。

但走出來之後，
那條路就會變成路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合2026畢業生發限動或寫在畢業卡片上——把「沒人帶、沒人懂」這種焦慮從劣勢翻成「我們是開路的人」的世代敘事，比假裝樂觀更有力
variations: 我們這屆沒有教戰守則，因為教戰守則要等我們畢業之後才寫得出來。
AI不是來搶我們飯碗的，是來逼我們去想：什麼東西，只有我們做得到。

en.content:
We're the class
that got chased to the finish line by AI.

In school, professors said: "Don't use it."
In interviews, managers ask: "How well do you use it?"

Nobody handed us a playbook,
because the playbook is the thing we're writing as we go, mostly by falling first.

So if you feel lost right now —
that's accurate.
The first people walking a path nobody walked
never get GPS.

But once we walk it,
it becomes a path.
en.editorNote: Built for 2026 grads to post or drop into a graduation card — reframes the 'no one taught us this' anxiety as a generational origin story instead of forcing fake optimism
variations: Our class didn't get a manual. The manual gets written after we graduate.
AI isn't here to take our jobs. It's here to force us to figure out what only we can do.

items.sourceNote: 受2026最紅的「In my ___ era」caption趨勢啟發，做成台灣畢業生版的時期切換清單

zh-tw.content:
正式宣布：

「期中考前一天才開始念書」時期：結束。
「報告寫到天亮然後睡到下午」時期：結束。
「三餐靠便利商店和泡麵」時期：結束。
「裝懂Reference但其實在Google」時期：結束。

「不知道下個月薪水會不會發」時期：開始。

換湯不換藥，但很值得拍照紀念。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照IG caption首選——抓住Z世代愛用的「era切換」格式，最後一句反轉拉出共鳴，比單純列「終於畢業了」清單有梗很多
variations: 結束一個時期叫畢業，開始下一個時期叫社畜。
畢業就是把舊的「裝沒事」換成新的「裝沒事」。

en.content:
Officially announcing:

"Started studying the night before the midterm" era: ended.
"Wrote the paper till sunrise, slept till sunset" era: ended.
"Ate exclusively from the convenience store" era: ended.
"Pretended to know the reading, secretly Googling it" era: ended.

"Not sure if next month's paycheck will hit" era: beginning.

Different decade, same chaos. Worth a photo dump though.
en.editorNote: Built for the grad photo carousel caption — leans on the Gen Z 'era' format and twists the last line for the punchline, way more shareable than a generic 'finally graduated' list
variations: Ending one era is called graduation. Starting the next one is called full-time employment.
Graduation is just trading one version of 'pretending I'm fine' for a new one.

items.sourceNote: 受Hilary Duff 2026 Northeastern畢業演講「you have to be who you are because everyone else is taken」啟發，重寫成更具體的台灣場景版

zh-tw.content:
出社會之後，
會有人很有禮貌地建議你：

「你不要那麼直接，學一下圓融。」
「你不要那麼安靜，學一下會講話。」
「你不要那麼認真，學一下混。」

你可以參考。
但不要全收。

因為「圓融、會講、會混」的人，
這個世界已經有十億個了。

你之所以是你，
是因為剩下那幾個怪怪的、刪不掉的特質。

那不是bug，
那是你出廠設定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業卡片、演講結尾、或寫給剛進職場一年內的學弟妹——比「做自己」三個字具體很多，把「被改造的壓力」翻成「保留出廠設定」更有畫面
variations: 別把自己改到讓主管滿意，最後連你媽都認不出來。
你身上那些別人想幫你「修掉」的部分，通常就是你最值錢的部分。

en.content:
Once you start working,
people will politely suggest:

"Don't be so blunt — learn to be smoother."
"Don't be so quiet — learn to speak up."
"Don't take it so seriously — learn to coast."

Feel free to consider it.
Don't accept all of it.

The world already has a billion smooth, well-spoken, coasting people.

You're you because of the few weird, un-edit-able traits left over.

That's not a bug.
That's your factory setting.
en.editorNote: Lands well in graduation cards, end-of-speech sign-offs, or notes to friends one year into their first job — way more concrete than 'be yourself' and reframes 'being shaped by the workplace' as protecting your factory settings
variations: Don't tune yourself until your boss is happy and your own mom can't recognize you anymore.
The parts of you other people keep trying to 'fix' are usually the parts that are actually worth something.

items.sourceNote: 受graduation picture caption「the journey not the destination」概念啟發，改寫成台灣大學生具體的四年時間軸

zh-tw.content:
四年是怎麼過去的？

是一張悠遊卡的餘額，從常常忘記儲值，到嗶下去那一刻才發現要畢業了。

是一台筆電的開機畫面，從新生訓練那天的怦然，到最後一份報告交出去那天的疲倦。

是宿舍床鋪上那條棉被，從第一次媽媽幫你鋪的緊張，到自己亂塞進行李袋的不捨。

是球場、是教室、是宵夜攤、是那個你以為會走一輩子的人。

四年怎麼過去的？

就是被一千個小細節，輕輕推完的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業前夕、畢業典禮當天、或畢業旅行結束時發長文——用具體物件當時間軸比寫「時光飛逝」有感覺一百倍，每個細節都能勾出讀者自己的版本
variations: 四年不是過去的，是被無數個「明天再說」累積成「原來已經是最後一天」的。
那些當下覺得很瑣碎的小事，後來才發現，那就是大學。

en.content:
How did four years go by?

It's the balance on a transit card — from always forgetting to top it up, to the second the gate beeps and you realize you're about to graduate.

It's a laptop boot screen — from the giddy click on orientation day, to the exhausted click submitting the final paper.

It's the blanket on the dorm bed — from the nervous way mom tucked it in on day one, to the way you just shoved it into a duffel on the way out.

It's a court, a classroom, a late-night food stall, the person you were sure you'd walk through life with.

How did four years go by?

A thousand tiny details, quietly nudging it forward.
en.editorNote: Built for the night before graduation, the day of the ceremony, or the end of your senior trip — using specific objects as a timeline hits a hundred times harder than writing 'time flies', and every detail will pull a memory out of whoever's reading
variations: Four years didn't pass. They got accumulated, one 'I'll deal with it tomorrow' at a time, until tomorrow turned out to be the last day.
All those tiny things that felt like nothing at the time? Turns out, that was college.

items.sourceNote: 受2026年最熱的「Plot twist: I graduated」短句caption趨勢啟發，改寫成台灣大學生版的劇情反轉

zh-tw.content:
劇情大反轉：

那個大一被當三科、
大二退選四門、
大三開始懷疑人生、
大四交報告交到凌晨四點、
還在想自己是不是讀錯科系的人，

居然——

畢業了。

編劇你是不是寫錯了？
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照IG caption超適用，跟著「Plot twist: I graduated」短句格式走，但加入台灣大學生具體經歷讓共鳴瞬間爆表
variations: Plot twist：那個大學四年都在邊緣求生的我，今天穿學士服了。
我以為是悲劇片，結果最後一集劇情反轉，我畢業了。

en.content:
Plot twist:

The one who failed three classes freshman year,
dropped four courses sophomore year,
started questioning life junior year,
turned in the final paper at 4am senior year,
and kept wondering if they picked the wrong major —

actually,

graduated.

Writers' room, was that intentional?
en.editorNote: Built for the grad photo Instagram caption — rides the 2026 'Plot twist: I graduated' short-caption trend but loads it with specific student-life beats so it hits way harder than the generic version
variations: Plot twist: the kid who barely survived four years of college is wearing a cap and gown today.
Thought I was in a tragedy. Turns out the season finale was a graduation.

items.sourceNote: 受Hilary Duff 2026 Northeastern畢業演講「you're building a life, not just a career」啟發，重寫成更接地氣的提醒

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，
大家會問你「找到工作沒」、
「薪水多少」、
「打算考研嗎」、
「要不要回家」。

沒有人會問你：

「你最近過得開心嗎？」
「你還記得自己想成為什麼樣的人嗎？」
「你有沒有為了自己活，而不是為了別人的問題活？」

履歷會繼續長，
薪水會繼續變，
但「你是誰」這件事，
沒有人會幫你寫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業卡片、學長姐寫給學弟妹的留言、或畢業典禮致詞——把「規劃人生」翻譯成「不要被別人的問題綁架」，更貼近剛畢業的迷茫感
variations: 別人問的是你的履歷，但你要自己問你的人生。
畢業之後最重要的問題，從來不是別人問你的那幾個。

en.content:
After graduation,
people will ask if you've found a job,
how much it pays,
whether you're going to grad school,
whether you're moving back home.

Nobody will ask:

"Are you actually happy lately?"
"Do you still remember who you wanted to be?"
"Are you living for yourself, or just answering everyone else's questions?"

Your resume will keep growing.
Your salary will keep changing.
But "who you are" —
nobody else is going to write that part for you.
en.editorNote: Lands well in graduation cards, notes from older friends to new grads, or speech sign-offs — reframes 'plan your life' as 'don't get hijacked by other people's questions', which speaks directly to post-grad confusion
variations: Other people are asking about your resume. You have to ask about your life.
The most important questions after graduation are never the ones anyone else asks you.

items.sourceNote: 受「Adulting unlocked. Please send help」短caption啟發，重寫成台灣畢業生版的成就解鎖清單

zh-tw.content:
成就解鎖：大人。

附贈技能：
☑ 自己繳水電費
☑ 假裝看得懂勞健保扣繳
☑ 開會時點頭裝懂
☑ 一個月最多去家裡兩次
☑ 凌晨兩點才睡但隔天還是要上班

剩餘血量：12%
求支援。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業後一個月內傳給朋友最有共鳴，也很適合貼到IG限動配上一張累累的自拍——「成就解鎖」格式讓抱怨變得好笑而不是悲情
variations: 大人成就解鎖。隨之而來的還有：腰痠、失眠、和對週一的恐懼。
解鎖大人技能的同時，也解鎖了「為什麼以前覺得放假很無聊」這個謎題。

en.content:
Achievement unlocked: adult.

Skills included:
☑ Paying my own utility bill
☑ Pretending to read my pay stub
☑ Nodding in meetings like I get it
☑ Visiting home maybe twice a month
☑ Going to bed at 2am, still showing up at 9

Remaining HP: 12%
Requesting backup.
en.editorNote: Hits hardest one month into post-grad life, perfect for an IG story over an exhausted selfie — the 'achievement unlocked' frame turns complaining into a joke instead of a sob story
variations: Adult mode unlocked. Side effects: back pain, insomnia, and a deep fear of Mondays.
Unlocked adulthood. Also unlocked the mystery of why I ever thought weekends off were boring.

items.sourceNote: 受短caption「Hard work pays off. Also a lot of luck」啟發，擴寫成台灣畢業生版誠實版謝詞

zh-tw.content:
畢業感言誠實版：

70%是運氣。
20%是身邊的人撐我。
8%是當初亂選那堂課剛好不難。
剩下2%才是我自己努力。

所以接下來這份畢業證書，
不只是發給我，
也要分給：
借我筆記的同學、
陪我熬夜的朋友、
沒過問成績的家人、
以及那位給C的教授——

沒有你們，
我可能還在重修。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮後傳給家人朋友、寫在畢業卡片內頁、或當IG長文caption都很適合——比正式感謝詞真實很多，也更暖，把「我自己很厲害」翻成「我很幸運」反而更有後勁
variations: 畢業典禮上沒講出口的話：謝謝那些不是因為我才畢業、卻讓我能畢業的人。
我的學位證書應該是合資的，每一個幫過我的人都該分一份。

en.content:
Honest graduation speech:

70% was luck.
20% was the people who carried me.
8% was that one easy elective I picked by accident.
The remaining 2% was me actually trying.

So this diploma
isn't really just mine.
It also belongs to:
the classmate who lent me their notes,
the friend who pulled all-nighters with me,
the family who never asked about my GPA,
and the professor who gave me a C —

Without all of you,
I'd probably still be retaking it.
en.editorNote: Perfect for sending to family and friends after the ceremony, writing inside a graduation card, or as a long-form IG caption — way more honest than a formal thank-you, and reframing 'I worked hard' as 'I got lucky' actually lands warmer
variations: What I didn't say at the graduation ceremony: thank you to the people who didn't graduate but somehow made sure I did.
My diploma should really be a group purchase. Everyone who helped me deserves a piece.

items.sourceNote: 受短caption「The tassel was worth the hassle」啟發，重寫成台灣畢業生內心戲版本

zh-tw.content:
為了這條流蘇，

我熬了三百多個夜，
喝了不知道幾杯便利商店咖啡，
報告寫到天亮，
小組討論變成獨角戲，
還跟教授解釋過為什麼缺席。

現在這條流蘇從左邊撥到右邊，
只用了三秒。

但說實話——
值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天最適合貼這篇——對應典禮上「撥穗」那一刻的內心OS，把三秒的儀式翻譯成四年的代價，比「我畢業了」更有畫面感
variations: 撥穗只要三秒，但我為了這三秒準備了四年。
流蘇從左到右，是這四年我走過最短、也最重的一段路。

en.content:
For this little tassel,

I pulled three hundred all-nighters,
drank an unholy amount of convenience store coffee,
finished group projects mostly alone,
and made up excuses to professors more times than I'll admit.

Now this tassel moves from left to right
in three seconds.

But honestly —
worth it.
en.editorNote: Post-it on graduation day for the moment the tassel flips — turns three seconds of ceremony into four years of receipts, hits harder than just 'I graduated'
variations: The tassel flips in three seconds. I spent four years getting ready for those three seconds.
Left to right — the shortest and heaviest walk of the last four years.

items.sourceNote: 受「Educated… but still figuring life out」短caption啟發，擴寫成更貼近畢業後迷茫的長版

zh-tw.content:
讀了十六年書，
學會了：

微積分、
光合作用、
第一次世界大戰的起因、
還有怎麼用Excel做樞紐分析。

沒學會的：

怎麼報稅、
怎麼跟房東談價錢、
怎麼煮一鍋不會燒焦的飯、
還有「現在到底要幹嘛」。

但沒關係，
反正畢業證書上面也沒寫這些。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業後一週內最有共鳴——把「學歷」和「生活技能」的落差寫成清單，比單純抱怨「學校沒教」更有梗，也很適合IG長文caption
variations: 畢業證書證明我讀過書，但沒人跟我保證我會生活。
學校教我光合作用，但沒教我怎麼讓自己不枯萎。

en.content:
Sixteen years of school taught me:

calculus,
photosynthesis,
the causes of World War I,
and how to make a pivot table.

Things school did not teach me:

how to file taxes,
how to negotiate with a landlord,
how to cook rice without burning it,
and what I'm supposed to do now.

But whatever —
none of that was on the diploma anyway.
en.editorNote: Most relatable in the first week post-grad — listing the gap between 'what school taught' and 'what life needs' lands sharper than just complaining, and works as a long-form IG caption
variations: My diploma proves I studied. It does not, however, guarantee I can adult.
School taught me photosynthesis but not how to keep myself from wilting.

items.sourceNote: 受短caption「Chapter closed」啟發，擴寫成更有情感層次的告別篇

zh-tw.content:
這一章，
關起來了。

不是用力摔上的那種，
是慢慢闔上、
還會回頭看一眼的那種。

書還在，
書頁也還會翻，
只是接下來會出現的角色，
不一定有現在這些。

但謝謝這幾頁，
寫得很用力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮前後傳給最好的那群朋友最有感——把「畢業」翻成「闔上一章」的比喻很經典，加上「不一定有現在這些角色」直接戳中分離的不捨
variations: 這一章不是壞掉了才結束，是因為它已經寫得夠好了。
翻到下一頁之前，請允許我多看這一頁幾秒。

en.content:
This chapter
is closed.

Not slammed shut.
The kind that closes slowly,
with one last look back.

The book is still here.
The pages will still turn.
It's just that the characters in the next chapter
won't all be the same ones.

But thank you to these pages —
they were written with everything I had.
en.editorNote: Lands best when sent to your closest group around the ceremony — the 'closing a chapter' metaphor is classic, but 'the characters won't all be the same' is the line that breaks people
variations: This chapter didn't end because it broke. It ended because it was good enough.
Before I turn the page, let me look at this one for a few more seconds.

items.sourceNote: 受「No cap, I graduated」短caption啟發，融合台灣社群梗重寫成「真的假的」系列

zh-tw.content:
我畢業了。

真的假的？
真的。
你確定？
我也很意外。

那是不是要慶祝？
不是，是要找工作。

那找到了沒？
沒有。

所以你現在是？
畢業生。

那跟學生差在哪？
差在沒有寒暑假，
但其他都一樣不確定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業後一個月內傳給朋友超有共鳴——用對話體把「畢業」、「找工作」、「沒目標」的尷尬全部攤出來，比單純的抱怨好笑多了，也很適合貼到限動
variations: 畢業生的定義：拿著一張紙，站在原地，看著大家走來走去。
從學生畢業之後，我以為會升級，結果只是換成另一種沒有寒暑假的不確定。

en.content:
I graduated.

For real?
For real.
You sure?
Honestly, I'm surprised too.

So are we celebrating?
No, we're job hunting.

Found anything?
No.

So what are you, exactly?
A graduate.

How's that different from a student?
No more summer break.
Everything else is just as unclear.
en.editorNote: Hits hard within a month of graduating — using dialogue format to expose the awkwardness of 'graduated, no job, no plan' is way funnier than just complaining, perfect for a story post
variations: Definition of a graduate: holding a piece of paper, standing still, watching everyone else move.
I thought graduating from being a student was a level-up. Turns out it's just a different kind of uncertainty, minus summer break.

items.sourceNote: 綜合多個短caption，重寫成「給未來的自己」式畢業留言

zh-tw.content:
寫給五年後的自己：

如果你正在加班，
請記得二十二歲那天，
你穿著畢業服站在校門口，
以為人生會很順。

如果你正在懷疑自己，
請記得那天你拍的那張團體照，
每個人都笑得很用力，
那不是裝的。

如果你過得不錯，
那也很好。
替今天的我謝謝你，
沒有把那個傻傻的、
還相信很多事的自己，
弄丟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天或前一晚最適合寫——用「寫給未來的自己」的視角，把「畢業祝福」翻譯成「拜託別把現在的我弄丟」，比直接喊勵志口號更深刻、也更有後勁
variations: 畢業這天我最大的願望，是五年後的我還認得今天的我。
願你以後再忙再累，都還記得有一天你笑得那麼用力，而且那不是為了任何人。

en.content:
To myself, five years from now:

If you're working late,
remember the day you were twenty-two,
standing outside school in your gown,
thinking life would just sort itself out.

If you're doubting yourself,
remember that group photo you took,
where everyone smiled hard.
None of it was fake.

If you're doing okay,
that's good too.
From me right now — thank you,
for not losing
the slightly naive version of me
who still believed in a lot of things.
en.editorNote: Best written the night before or the day of the ceremony — using a 'letter to future self' frame turns a graduation message into 'please don't lose who I am right now', which lasts longer than any motivational quote
variations: My biggest wish on graduation day is that five years from now, I still recognize the person I was today.
However tired you get, please remember the day you smiled that hard — and that you weren't doing it for anyone else.

items.sourceNote: 受「In my graduated era」短caption啟發，擴寫成自嘲版「畢業時代」

zh-tw.content:
正式進入「我畢業了」時代。

這個時代的特徵是：
睡覺前會擔心明天要做什麼，
但起床後又不想做。

履歷投了，沒回。
朋友散了，沒事。
夢想呢？
夢想還在，只是搬到比較遠的地方住。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業後一兩個月最有共鳴——把「畢業時代」翻成「焦慮但又懶」的反差最好笑，貼限動配畢業照保證有人留言「這就是我」
variations: 畢業時代開啟：人生模式從「按表操課」改成「按心情擺爛」。
我的畢業時代不是新章節，是上一章還沒寫完，只是被翻過去了。

en.content:
Officially in my graduated era.

Defining traits of this era:
worrying about what to do tomorrow before bed,
and then not wanting to do any of it after waking up.

Applications sent. No replies.
Friends scattered. It's fine.
Dreams?
Still alive, just living a little further away now.
en.editorNote: Lands hardest a month or two post-grad — the contrast between 'anxious' and 'lazy' inside one 'era' is the joke, perfect for a story post with a graduation photo
variations: Graduated era unlocked: life mode switched from 'follow the schedule' to 'follow the vibes (poorly)'.
My graduated era isn't a new chapter. It's the previous one, unfinished, just flipped past.

items.sourceNote: 受「Shoutout to Google. This was a long group project」啟發，重寫成台灣版感謝清單

zh-tw.content:
畢業感謝名單：

感謝Google，
沒有你我畢不了業。
感謝ChatGPT，
讓報告長出比我還聰明的句子。
感謝那位每次都把作業傳到群組的同學，
你才是這屆真正的MVP。
感謝便利商店，
是我深夜趕報告的精神支柱。

最後感謝我自己，
沒有在過程中徹底放棄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮後或拍完畢業照貼最適合——用「感謝名單」的格式列出大家都靠過但不敢明講的東西，比假掰致詞好笑十倍
variations: 我能畢業是團體合作的成果，只是團體成員大部分不是人。
畢業證書應該要附上：Google、ChatGPT、和那位很佛的同學。

en.content:
Graduation thank-you list:

Thanks to Google.
Without you, I would not be here.
Thanks to ChatGPT,
for producing sentences smarter than I am.
Thanks to that one classmate
who always dropped the assignment into the group chat —
you are the real MVP of this class.
Thanks to the convenience store,
for emotionally supporting me at 2am.

And finally, thanks to me
for not fully giving up halfway.
en.editorNote: Post-ceremony or with cap-and-gown photos this hits — formatting it as a 'thank-you list' for the things everyone secretly relied on is funnier than any sincere speech
variations: Graduating was a group effort. Most of the group wasn't human.
My diploma should come with footnotes: Google, ChatGPT, and that one really kind classmate.

items.sourceNote: 受「Late nights created bright futures」啟發，寫成回望版的深夜獨白

zh-tw.content:
那些沒睡的晚上，
後來都變成了今天。

圖書館關門前的最後一杯咖啡，
趕報告趕到天亮的那雙眼睛，
哭著問自己「我到底為了什麼」的那個凌晨——

那時候不知道，
那些其實都不是浪費。

只是還沒有人告訴你：
你撐過的每一個晚上，
都會有一個白天，
替你發光。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮前晚或畢業當天最有後勁——把「熬夜」這種負面回憶翻譯成「今天的光」很有力量，適合長文發限動或寫給自己
variations: 你以為那些夜晚浪費了你，其實是它們在養你。
今天會發光，是因為那些沒睡的晚上把光存起來了。

en.content:
All those nights you didn't sleep
turned into today.

The last coffee before the library closed,
the eyes that watched the sky go from black to grey,
the 4am where you asked yourself what any of this was for —

you didn't know it then,
but none of it was wasted.

Nobody had told you yet:
for every night you got through,
there would be a day
that shines on your behalf.
en.editorNote: Best the night before or the day of the ceremony — reframing 'sleepless nights' into 'stored-up light' has weight, works as a long caption or a note to yourself
variations: You thought those nights were draining you. They were feeding you.
Today shines because the nights you didn't sleep were saving up the light.

items.sourceNote: 受短caption「Now accepting job offers」啟發，擴寫成台灣求職焦慮版對話

zh-tw.content:
畢業後第三天。

媽：「找工作了沒？」
我：「找了。」
媽：「找到了沒？」
我：「沒。」
媽：「那你在幹嘛？」
我：「等回信。」
媽：「等多久了？」
我：「兩個禮拜。」
媽：「……」
我：「……」

沉默是這個夏天最常見的對話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給也剛畢業在找工作的朋友，或是貼限動讓所有應屆畢業生集體共鳴——對話體把「家人關心」和「自己焦慮」的雙重壓力一次寫出來，比抱怨真實多了
variations: 畢業後最常出現的兩個句子：「找了」跟「沒消息」。
求職的本質是：你寄出去的信比你收到的多很多。

en.content:
Day three after graduation.

Mom: "Looking for work?"
Me: "Yes."
Mom: "Found anything?"
Me: "No."
Mom: "So what are you doing?"
Me: "Waiting for replies."
Mom: "How long?"
Me: "Two weeks."
Mom: "..."
Me: "..."

Silence is this summer's most frequent conversation.
en.editorNote: Send to a fellow fresh grad who's job hunting, or post as a story for collective fresh-grad solidarity — the dialogue format captures both 'family pressure' and 'personal anxiety' in one go, way more honest than a regular vent
variations: The two most common sentences after graduating: 'I applied' and 'No reply yet.'
Job hunting, in essence: the emails you send vastly outnumber the ones you get back.

items.sourceNote: 受Arthur Ashe「Start where you are」啟發，重新寫成現實版台灣語感

zh-tw.content:
從你站著的地方開始就好。

不用先準備好，
不用先看清楚，
不用先有把握。

手上有什麼用什麼，
會什麼做什麼，
剩下的，邊走邊學。

人生不是先及格才能上場，
是上場才會慢慢及格。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業後第一份工作前夕、或是覺得自己「還沒準備好」的時候給自己看，比一句空泛的「加油」實在多了
variations: 你不用先變成那個你想成為的人，才開始往那邊走。
準備好再開始，那永遠不會開始。

en.content:
Start from wherever you're standing.

You don't have to be ready.
You don't have to see the whole road.
You don't have to be sure.

Use what's in your hands.
Do what you can do.
The rest, you'll figure out on the way.

Life isn't pass-the-test-then-play.
It's play first, pass eventually.
en.editorNote: Save this for the night before your first job or when 'I'm not ready yet' loops in your head — way more useful than a generic 'you got this'
variations: You don't have to become the person you want to be before you start walking that way.
If you wait until you're ready, you'll never start.

items.sourceNote: 受短caption「I would like to thank Google and Quizlet」啟發，擴寫成台灣學生致謝清單

zh-tw.content:
畢業致謝名單：

感謝 Google，
感謝 ChatGPT，
感謝 PTT 學長姐心得，
感謝期末前一週才分享筆記的同學，
感謝凌晨還開的便利商店，
感謝我那台快壞掉但還沒壞的筆電。

以及——
感謝那個沒放棄交報告的我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天貼IG超適合，把「正式致謝」翻譯成大學生真正會感謝的東西，最後一句反差讓整段不只是搞笑
variations: 如果畢業證書要列共同作者，至少要分一半給 Google。
感謝那個明知會通宵還是按下「再看一集」的我，最後也是你撐到畢業的。

en.content:
Acknowledgments page:

Thank you, Google.
Thank you, ChatGPT.
Thank you, that one Reddit thread from 2019.
Thank you, the classmate who shared notes the night before finals.
Thank you, the convenience store that stayed open at 3am.
Thank you, my laptop that has been dying for two years.

And —
thank you, the version of me
that kept hitting submit.
en.editorNote: Drop this as a graduation day caption — turns the 'thank you speech' into the things students actually relied on, and the last line lifts it from joke to something real
variations: If diplomas listed co-authors, Google would get half the credit.
Thanks to the version of me who knew it was a bad idea to start another episode and started it anyway — somehow you still made it here.

items.sourceNote: 受台灣傳統畢業祝福句啟發，重新寫成不肉麻的版本

zh-tw.content:
我不會跟你說什麼鵬程萬里、
前途無量，
那種話太遠了，講出來都不像在說你。

我只想說：

你以後不管在哪裡、做什麼、
過得好不好——

如果有一天累到想找一個人講話，
記得我還在。

畢業快樂。
你不用一個人扛全部。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給最熟的那一兩個朋友，比制式祝福更觸動，特別是寫在卡片或畢業留言裡——重點是「我還在」這個承諾
variations: 祝你前程似錦這種話我說不出口，但我會接你電話，這比較實在。
你不用變得多厲害，我都一樣會覺得你很好。

en.content:
I'm not going to tell you you're going to do amazing things
or that the world is yours.
That kind of talk is too far away — it doesn't even sound like you.

What I want to say is:

Wherever you end up,
whatever you're doing,
whether you're okay or not —

if there's a day you're too tired and just need someone to talk to,
remember I'm still here.

Happy graduation.
You don't have to carry all of it alone.
en.editorNote: Write this in a card to your closest friend, or send as a long DM — works because it skips the inspirational stuff and lands on the one thing that actually matters: I'm still here
variations: I can't really pull off 'go conquer the world,' but I can pick up when you call. That's the realer version.
You don't have to become someone impressive. I'd think you were great anyway.

items.sourceNote: 受「end of one chapter, start of next」概念啟發，改寫成更真實的中間狀態

zh-tw.content:
畢業這件事最怪的地方是：

上一個章節結束了，
但下一個章節還沒開始。

你站在中間那一頁——
空白的、沒有字、沒有人告訴你要寫什麼。

第一次，沒有人發進度給你，
沒有 deadline，
也沒有「下學期會更好」這種藉口。

那一頁，要你自己寫。
慢慢寫沒關係。
寫錯也沒關係。
但別空著太久。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼給畢業後不知道要幹嘛的朋友，或是發限動給同樣在迷茫的同屆——說中那個「卡在中間」的真實感受，又給了一點推力
variations: 你不是迷路，你是在還沒寫到的那一頁。
畢業後最難的不是新階段，是中間那一段沒人告訴你怎麼過的空白。

en.content:
The strangest thing about graduating is this:
